summary: Wired macOS notarization (afterSign hook + notarytool API key) and proved it end-to-end locally: build → sign (keychain Developer ID) → afterSign notarize submit → staple → spctl 'accepted, source=Notarized Developer ID'. One repair cycle: the first API key tried (ApiKey_R9WYMTP5I5DS, an App Store Connect *Individual* key) returned HTTP 401 against the team Issuer ID; switching to a *Team* key (AuthKey_9XHDX3ZN44) authenticated and notarization succeeded. All 5 GitHub secrets set (the 2 notary secrets updated to the working team key).

criteria_results.text: afterSign hook notarizes + staples when creds present, no-ops locally otherwise
criteria_results.notes: Hook ran, submitted, stapled; no-op path verified earlier without creds.

criteria_results.text: Packaged .app passes Gatekeeper as Notarized Developer ID
criteria_results.notes: stapler validate OK; spctl -a -> accepted, source=Notarized Developer ID.

process_issues.description: Release/deploy credential mismatch: an App Store Connect *Individual* key (ApiKey_ prefix) was tried first and 401'd against the team Issuer ID; notarization requires a *Team* key with >= Developer role. Lesson now in docs/release-notarization.md troubleshooting. Verify keys with `notarytool history` before wiring secrets.

observed_chain: Pre-Flight #4 static release audit (found 0 secrets + custom-sign-hook gap)
